message:
feat(ft-api,ft-kernel-cpu,ft-autograd): add sparse tensor operations (COO/CSR)

Introduce sparse tensor support across the frankentorch stack:

ft-api (FrankenTorchSession):
- sparse_coo_tensor(): create COO sparse tensor from coordinates and values
- sparse_coo_tensor_from_indices(): create from indices/values tensors
- sparse_csr_tensor(): create CSR sparse tensor from crow_indices/col_indices
- sparse_to_dense(): convert any sparse tensor to dense
- sparse_coo_matmul(): sparse-dense matrix multiply via kernel dispatch

ft-kernel-cpu:
- sparse_coo_matmul_dense_f64(): sparse [M,K] @ dense [K,N] -> dense [M,N]
  with full dimension validation and bounds checking
- sparse_coo_add_dense_f64(): element-wise sparse + dense addition
- sparse_coo_coalesce_f64(): coalesce duplicate indices by summing values
- sparse_csr_matmul_dense_f64(): CSR sparse-dense matrix multiply
- sparse_csr_to_coo_f64(): CSR to COO format conversion
- Comprehensive test suite for all sparse operations

ft-autograd:
- Add SparseTensor(SparseTensorError) variant to AutogradError
- Implement From<SparseTensorError> for AutogradError conversion

Update beads issue tracker.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

-{"id":"frankentorch-0tw","title":"Add sparse tensor storage and basic sparse ops","description":"PyTorch's sparse tensor support (COO and CSR formats) is used for embedding layers, graph neural networks, and sparse linear algebra. FrankenTorch currently has no sparse representation.

Implementation plan:
1. Add SparseCOOTensor to ft-core:
   - indices: DenseTensor of shape [sparse_dim, nnz] (i64)
   - values: DenseTensor of shape [nnz, *dense_dims]
   - shape: the full dense shape
   - coalesced flag
2. Add SparseCSRTensor to ft-core:
   - crow_indices: Vec<i64> (compressed row pointers)
   - col_indices: Vec<i64>
   - values: DenseTensor
3. Add sparse creation functions to ft-api:
   - session.sparse_coo_tensor(indices, values, shape)
   - session.to_sparse() / session.to_dense()
4. Add basic sparse ops in ft-kernel-cpu:
   - sparse-dense matmul (the highest-value op for embeddings)
   - sparse addition (coalesce + merge)
   - sparse_coo to dense conversion
5. Integrate with Embedding: optionally use sparse gradients for large vocabularies

Tests:
- Unit test: COO tensor creation, to_dense round-trip
- Unit test: CSR tensor creation, to_dense round-trip
- Unit test: sparse-dense matmul correctness vs dense matmul
- Unit test: coalesce merges duplicate indices
- Unit test: sparse addition
- E2E test: Embedding with sparse gradients for SparseAdam","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-07T04:18:24.369221708Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-07T04:18:24.369221708Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"frankentorch-0tw","title":"Add sparse tensor storage and basic sparse ops","description":"## Progress

### Completed
1. SparseCOOTensor in ft-core with indices [sparse_dim, nnz], values [nnz, *dense_dims], coalesced flag
2. SparseCSRTensor in ft-core with crow_indices, col_indices, values
3. SparseTensorError enum with comprehensive validation errors
4. AutogradError::SparseTensor variant for error propagation
5. ft-api sparse tensor methods:
   - sparse_coo_tensor(coords, values, shape, dtype)
   - sparse_coo_tensor_from_indices(indices, values, shape, sparse_dim, coalesced)
   - sparse_csr_tensor(crow_indices, col_indices, values, shape)
   - tensor_to_sparse_coo(input) - dense to sparse conversion
   - sparse_coo_to_dense / sparse_csr_to_dense
6. ft-kernel-cpu sparse operations:
   - sparse_coo_matmul_dense_f64 - sparse @ dense matmul
   - sparse_coo_coalesce - merge duplicate indices
   - sparse_coo_add - element-wise addition

### Tests (11 passing)
- 8 in ft-core: COO/CSR creation, to_dense roundtrip, error handling
- 3 in ft-kernel-cpu: matmul, coalesce, add

### Remaining
- Embedding layer sparse gradient integration
- SparseAdam optimizer support","status":"in_progress","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-07T04:18:24.369221708Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-10T03:01:48.607850715Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankentorch-18a","title":"Align proposed architecture crate map with current workspace layers","description":"Update PROPOSED_ARCHITECTURE.md so its crate map and runtime flow mention the in-tree ft-nn, ft-optim, and ft-data layers instead of stopping at ft-api/ft-conformance.","notes":"Updated the proposed architecture doc so the crate map includes ft-nn, ft-optim, and ft-data, and the runtime flow now acknowledges those higher-level layers before conformance/durability.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"docs","created_at":"2026-03-28T05:09:02.557560325Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-28T05:09:21.467027815Z","closed_at":"2026-03-28T05:09:21.466400527Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankentorch-1le","title":"Investigate persistent beads SQLite integrity warning after clean rebuild","description":"## Context
After a full non-destructive rebuild of `.beads/beads.db` from canonical `issues.jsonl`, `br doctor --json` still reports:

`sqlite.integrity_check: database disk image is malformed: page 17 is never used`

This persists even on the freshly rebuilt DB, while all operational checks are otherwise healthy (`br ready --json` is empty, sync metadata matches, blocked cache is not stale).

## Goal
Determine whether this warning reflects:
1. a genuine low-level SQLite file/layout problem,
2. expected frankensqlite behavior that `br doctor` should not flag as suspicious, or
3. a beads/br bug in the integrity-check path.

## Acceptance
- Reproduce the warning on the current canonical DB
- Compare against a freshly rebuilt temp DB
- Gather enough evidence to classify the issue as workspace-local vs tool-level
- If it is tool-level or not safely fixable from this repo, document the finding clearly and close or hand off with evidence","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-03T13:56:40.262095265Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-03T14:00:06.096361407Z","closed_at":"2026-04-03T14:00:06.096186135Z","close_reason":"Reproduced the integrity warning on canonical and rebuilt DBs, proved a plain SQLite VACUUM clears it, then observed br doctor --repair reintroduce the warning on a different page number while keeping logical metadata healthy; this classifies the issue as a tool-level beads/frankensqlite write-path problem rather than repo-local issue-data corruption","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankentorch-1s2","title":"Add F.pad reflect/replicate/circular modes","description":"Extend tensor_pad to support reflect, replicate, circular padding modes. Currently only constant padding. Essential for vision preprocessing.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-02T19:49:10.441649598Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-02T19:53:41.804652781Z","closed_at":"2026-04-02T19:53:41.804379905Z","close_reason":"Implemented tensor_pad_mode with reflect, replicate, circular modes. 6 tests passing.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
